subjects.name: Social Studies
subjects.description: Eritrean Grade 8 End-of-Basic Education National Examination covering History, Geography, Government, Economics, and Environment.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is WRONG about the location of Eritrea?

options.text: Eritrea is found north of the equator.
Eritrea is found to the west of the Red Sea.
Eritrea is found to the east of Sudan.
Eritrea has no border with Djibouti.

questions.explanation: Eritrea does share a border with Djibouti in the southeast.

questions.text: Between which latitudes is Eritrea located?
questions.explanation: Eritrea lies between 12°N and 18°N latitude.

questions.text: In which year did the Ottoman Turks occupy Massawa?
questions.explanation: The Ottoman Turks occupied Massawa in 1557.

questions.text: What was the capital of the medieval kingdom of Bahri Neghestat?
questions.explanation: Debarwa was the capital of the Bahri Neghestat (Sea Kingdom).

questions.text: Which UN resolution established the federation of Eritrea with Ethiopia?
questions.explanation: UN Resolution 390 A(V) of December 1950 established the Eritrea-Ethiopia federation.

questions.text: The Battle of Gura was the final defeat of which foreign force in Eritrea?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Gura (1876) was the final decisive defeat of the Egyptian invading forces.

questions.text: In which year was Mahber Fikri Hager founded?
questions.explanation: Mahber Fikri Hager (Love of Country Association) was founded in 1941 to oppose colonialism.

questions.text: Operation Fenkil, which liberated Massawa, took place in which year?
questions.explanation: Operation Fenkil took place in February 1990 and resulted in the liberation of Massawa.

questions.text: Which is the lowest stage of human social development?
questions.explanation: Primitive communal society is the lowest/earliest stage of human social development.

questions.text: In a water cycle diagram, if stage 1 is the process where water from lakes and oceans turns into vapour, what is stage 1?
questions.explanation: Evaporation is the process by which water from surface water bodies turns into water vapour.

questions.text: In which year was Italian Eritrea officially proclaimed?
questions.explanation: Italian Eritrea was officially proclaimed on January 1, 1890.

questions.text: Who fired the first shots of Eritrea's armed struggle for independence?
questions.explanation: Hamid Idris Awate fired the first shots on September 1, 1961, starting the armed struggle.

questions.text: When did the Eritrean independence referendum take place?
questions.explanation: The independence referendum was held on April 23–25, 1993, with 99.8% voting for independence.

questions.text: What is the highest mountain peak in Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Emba Soira (3,018 m) is the highest peak in Eritrea.

questions.text: How many ethnic groups are officially recognized in Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Eritrea officially recognizes 9 ethnic groups: Tigrinya, Tigre, Saho, Kunama, Rashaida, Bilen, Afar, Beja, and Nara.

questions.text: The EPLF was renamed to which organization in 1994?
questions.explanation: At the Third Congress in February 1994, the EPLF was renamed the People's Front for Democracy and Justice (PFDJ).

questions.text: On which date did the armed struggle for Eritrean independence begin?
questions.explanation: The armed struggle for independence began on September 1, 1961.

questions.text: Which Italian company first purchased the port of Assab in 1869?
questions.explanation: The Rubattino Shipping Company purchased Assab in 1869; the Italian government took control in 1882.

questions.text: What percentage of Eritreans voted for independence in the 1993 referendum?
questions.explanation: 99.8% of Eritreans voted for independence in the April 1993 referendum.

questions.text: In which year did Ethiopia annex Eritrea, abolishing the federation?
questions.explanation: Ethiopia formally annexed Eritrea on November 14, 1962, dissolving the federation.

questions.text: Which of the following is the largest administrative region (Zoba) in Eritrea by area?
questions.explanation: Gash-Barka is the largest zoba (region) in Eritrea by land area.

questions.text: Which battle in 1988 destroyed the Ethiopian Nadew Command?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Afabet in March 1988 destroyed the Ethiopian Nadew Command, a major turning point.

questions.text: When was Eritrea's constitution ratified?
questions.explanation: Eritrea's constitution was ratified by the Constituent Assembly on May 23, 1997.

questions.text: On a map with scale 1:50,000, a distance of 2 cm represents what actual distance?
What do contour lines on a topographic map connect?
questions.explanation: Contour lines connect points of equal elevation above sea level.

questions.text: Which of the following is the major island group of Eritrea?
questions.explanation: The Dahlak Archipelago, located in the Red Sea, is Eritrea's major island group.

questions.text: What is the capital city of Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Asmara is the capital city of Eritrea, situated at about 2,300 m above sea level.

questions.text: How many administrative regions (Zobas) does Eritrea have?
questions.explanation: Eritrea is divided into 6 administrative regions (Zobas).

questions.text: Which human development stage comes immediately after feudal society?
questions.explanation: The order is: Primitive communal → Slave → Feudal → Capitalist → Socialist.

questions.text: Which country borders Eritrea to the west and northwest?
questions.explanation: Sudan borders Eritrea to the west and northwest.

questions.text: The Eritrean Liberation Front (ELF) was founded in which year?
questions.explanation: The ELF (Eritrean Liberation Front) was founded in Cairo in 1960.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the main occupation of most Eritreans?
questions.explanation: Agriculture is the main occupation of approximately 80% of Eritrea's population.

questions.text: What is the major environmental problem threatening Eritrea's forests?
questions.explanation: Deforestation is the major environmental problem; forest cover has been drastically reduced.

questions.text: When did the British Administration of Eritrea begin?
questions.explanation: The British Administration of Eritrea began in 1941 after British and patriot forces defeated Italy.

questions.text: Which of the following is the major port city on Eritrea's southern Red Sea coast?
questions.explanation: Assab is the major port city on Eritrea's southern Red Sea coast.

questions.text: The ancient port of Adulis was the commercial center of which ancient kingdom?
questions.explanation: Adulis was the main commercial port of the Axumite Kingdom.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes a 'representative fraction' (RF) scale?

options.text: A scale shown as a line with measured distances
A scale shown as a fraction such as 1:25,000
A scale written in words such as '1 cm to 1 km'
A scale shown as a shaded area

questions.explanation: A representative fraction (RF) expresses scale as a ratio, e.g., 1:25,000.

questions.text: What was the name of the federation period during which Eritrea was linked with Ethiopia?
questions.explanation: Eritrea was federated with Ethiopia from 1952 until Ethiopia's annexation in 1962.

questions.text: Which of the following is a method used to conserve soil and prevent erosion on hillsides?
questions.explanation: Terracing creates flat steps on hillsides to slow water runoff and prevent soil erosion.

questions.text: What is the approximate total area of Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Eritrea has a total area of approximately 124,320 km².

questions.text: Between which longitudes is Eritrea located?
questions.explanation: Eritrea lies between 36°E and 44°E longitude.

questions.text: Which sea borders Eritrea to the east?
questions.explanation: The Red Sea borders Eritrea to the east.

questions.text: In which year did the Turks occupy Massawa?
questions.explanation: The Ottoman Turks occupied Massawa in 1557.

questions.text: What is the title of the ruler of Bahri Neghestat?
questions.explanation: The ruler of Bahri Neghestat (the Sea Kingdom) held the title Bahr Negash.

questions.text: Which country borders Eritrea to the south?
questions.explanation: Ethiopia borders Eritrea to the south.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a cause of deforestation in Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Planting trees (afforestation) is a solution to deforestation, not a cause of it.

questions.text: The Eritrean People's Liberation Front (EPLF) was formed in which year?
questions.explanation: The EPLF was formed in 1970 as a breakaway from the ELF.

questions.text: Which of the following is a renewable natural resource?
questions.explanation: Forests are renewable resources because they can be replanted and grow back.

questions.text: Eritrea's independence was officially declared on which date?
questions.explanation: Eritrea officially declared independence on May 24, 1993, after the referendum result.

questions.text: What was the result of the Battle of Adwa (1896)?

options.text: Italy defeated Ethiopia and took its territory.
Ethiopia defeated Italy, but Eritrea remained under Italian rule.
Italy and Ethiopia signed a peace treaty.
Eritrea was freed from Italian rule.

questions.explanation: Ethiopia defeated Italy at Adwa in 1896, but Eritrea remained under Italian colonial rule.

questions.text: Which of the following is the highest stage of human social development (according to the curriculum)?
questions.explanation: Socialist society is considered the highest stage in the developmental sequence taught in the curriculum.

questions.text: In a slave society, who owns the means of production?
questions.explanation: In a slave society, slave owners control the means of production and the labor of slaves.

questions.text: Which of the following is the smallest administrative region (Zoba) in Eritrea by area?
questions.explanation: Maekel (Central) Zoba is the smallest region by area, though it contains the capital Asmara.

questions.text: Which climate zone covers most of Eritrea's highland plateau?
questions.explanation: The highland plateau of Eritrea has a temperate climate due to its high elevation.

questions.text: Which of the following rivers is the longest in Eritrea?
questions.explanation: The Barka River is the longest river in Eritrea.

questions.text: What type of scale shows distance as a drawn line with measurements marked on it?
questions.explanation: A linear or bar scale shows distances as a drawn line with measurements, easy to use on a printed map.

questions.text: What does 'afforestation' mean?

options.text: Planting trees in areas that had few or no trees

questions.explanation: Afforestation is the process of planting trees to create new forests or restore degraded lands.

questions.text: Which organization did Mahber Fikri Hager oppose?
questions.explanation: Mahber Fikri Hager was established in 1941 to oppose Italian colonial rule and advocate for Eritrean rights.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes the term 'census'?
questions.explanation: A census is an official count or survey of all the people in a country at a specific time.

questions.text: Which of the following is a push factor that causes people to migrate from rural areas?
questions.explanation: Drought and lack of water are push factors that force people to leave rural areas.

questions.text: The Federation of Eritrea with Ethiopia came into effect in which year?
questions.explanation: The federation officially came into effect on September 11, 1952.

questions.text: Which of the following is the main staple grain crop grown in the Eritrean highlands?
questions.explanation: Teff is a major staple grain of the Eritrean highlands, used to make injera.

questions.text: Which of the following describes a 'non-renewable resource'?

options.text: A resource that can be replaced in a short time
A resource that cannot be replaced once used up

questions.explanation: Non-renewable resources (e.g., oil, coal, minerals) cannot be replaced once consumed.

questions.text: What is the main economic activity in Eritrea's coastal areas?
questions.explanation: Fishing is the main economic activity along Eritrea's Red Sea coast and Dahlak Islands.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about the Eritrean constitution of 1997 is correct?

options.text: It was ratified and implemented immediately.
It was ratified but not implemented.
It was rejected by the National Assembly.

questions.explanation: The 1997 constitution was ratified but has not been implemented.

questions.text: What is 'soil erosion'?

options.text: The addition of nutrients to the soil
The removal of topsoil by wind or water
The planting of crops to protect soil

questions.explanation: Soil erosion is the removal and displacement of the upper layer of soil by wind or water.

questions.text: Which of the following is a responsibility (duty) of an Eritrean citizen?
questions.explanation: Defending the country is one of the key duties of an Eritrean citizen.

questions.text: Which mineral resource is mined at the Bisha mine in Eritrea?
questions.explanation: The Bisha mine in Gash-Barka produces gold, copper, and zinc.

questions.text: What is the term for the movement of people from rural areas to urban areas?
questions.explanation: Rural-urban migration is the movement of people from the countryside to towns and cities.

questions.text: Which of the following describes 'desertification'?

options.text: The greening of arid land through irrigation
The process by which fertile land becomes desert

questions.explanation: Desertification is the process by which fertile or semi-arid land degrades into desert.

questions.text: What year did Italy occupy Massawa?
questions.explanation: Italy occupied the port of Massawa in February 1885.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes 'population density'?

options.text: The total number of people in a country
The number of people per unit area
The rate at which population grows
The number of births per year

questions.explanation: Population density is the number of people living per unit area (e.g., people per km²).

questions.text: Liberation of Asmara took place on which date?
questions.explanation: Asmara was liberated by the EPLF on May 24, 1991.

questions.text: Which of the following is a pull factor for migration to cities?

options.text: Lack of schools in rural areas

questions.explanation: Employment opportunities are a pull factor that attract people to urban areas.

questions.text: What is the main function of the National Assembly in Eritrea?
questions.explanation: The National Assembly is the legislative body responsible for making laws.

questions.text: How many continents are there in the world?
questions.explanation: There are 7 continents: Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, Australia/Oceania, and Antarctica.

questions.text: Which continent is Eritrea part of?
questions.explanation: Eritrea is located in the northeastern part of Africa, in the Horn of Africa region.

questions.text: What causes the formation of clouds in the water cycle?
questions.explanation: Condensation is the process where water vapour cools and forms water droplets, creating clouds.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct order of the water cycle?
questions.explanation: Water evaporates, rises and cools to condense into clouds, then falls as precipitation.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes the administrative hierarchy in Eritrea from largest to smallest?
questions.explanation: The hierarchy goes: Zoba (region) → Sub-zoba (district) → Village (community).

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a neighboring country of Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Kenya does not border Eritrea. Eritrea's neighbors are Sudan, Ethiopia, and Djibouti.

questions.text: Which Italian company originally purchased the port of Assab in 1869?
questions.explanation: The Rubattino Shipping Company purchased the port of Assab in 1869 from a local sultan.

questions.text: In which year was the Eritrean Liberation Front (ELF) founded?
questions.explanation: The ELF was founded in Cairo in 1960.

questions.text: What was the main reason the EPLF conducted its Strategic Withdrawal in 1978?

options.text: To regroup and avoid total defeat against a superior enemy force

questions.explanation: The EPLF's Strategic Withdrawal of 1978 was a tactical decision to avoid destruction and regroup in Sahel.

questions.text: The Eritrean highlands have which type of climate?
questions.explanation: The highland plateau of Eritrea enjoys a temperate climate due to its elevation of over 2,000 m.

questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of a feudal society?

options.text: All land is owned by the state
Land is owned by feudal lords and peasants work it in exchange for protection
Workers are legally owned by their employers
All citizens share equally in production

questions.explanation: In feudal society, lords own land and peasants/serfs work it in exchange for protection and use of the land.

questions.text: What is 'birth rate'?

options.text: The number of deaths per 1,000 people per year
The number of live births per 1,000 people per year
The total number of people in a country
The difference between births and deaths

questions.explanation: Birth rate is the number of live births per 1,000 people per year.

questions.text: In which year did Italy formally take government control of Assab from the Rubattino Company?
questions.explanation: The Italian government formally took control of Assab from the Rubattino Company in 1882.

questions.text: Which of the following are the two main religions practiced in Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Christianity and Islam are the two main religions, practiced by roughly equal portions of the population.

questions.text: What does the term 'natural increase' of population mean?

options.text: The difference between birth rate and death rate
Population growth due to better health care

questions.explanation: Natural increase is the difference between birth rate and death rate (without counting migration).

questions.text: The Battle of Gundet (1875) was fought against which invading force?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Gundet (1875) was a victory against Egyptian invading forces.

questions.text: Which of the following describes an 'archipelago'?

options.text: A chain or group of islands
A narrow strip of land connecting two larger landmasses

questions.explanation: An archipelago is a group or chain of islands scattered in a sea or ocean.

questions.text: What is Eritrea's coastline length approximately?
questions.explanation: Eritrea has a coastline of approximately 1,200 km along the Red Sea.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes 'subsistence farming'?

options.text: Farming that produces large surpluses for export
Farming that produces just enough food for the farmer's family
Farming using modern machinery and chemicals

questions.explanation: Subsistence farming produces just enough food to meet the needs of the farmer's household with little surplus.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a primary economic activity?
questions.explanation: Mining is a primary economic activity involving the extraction of natural resources.

questions.text: What does 'literacy rate' measure?

options.text: The number of schools in a country
The percentage of people who can read and write
The number of books published per year
The percentage of children attending school

questions.explanation: Literacy rate is the percentage of the population (usually aged 15 and above) that can read and write.

questions.text: How many oceans are there in the world?
questions.explanation: There are 5 oceans: Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, Southern (Antarctic), and Arctic.

questions.text: Which is Africa's largest ocean border?
questions.explanation: The Atlantic Ocean borders Africa to the west and is the largest ocean border of the African continent.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct definition of 'scale' on a map?

options.text: The ratio between distance on the map and the actual distance on the ground
The colors used on a map
The compass direction shown on a map

questions.explanation: Map scale is the ratio between a distance on the map and the corresponding actual distance on the ground.

questions.text: What is the name of the ancient port that was the main trade outlet for the Axumite Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Adulis, near present-day Zula, was the main commercial port of the Axumite Kingdom.

questions.text: What is the approximate elevation of Asmara above sea level?
questions.explanation: Asmara is situated at approximately 2,300 meters above sea level on the highland plateau.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct meaning of 'urbanization'?

options.text: The increase in the proportion of people living in urban areas
The movement of city people to rural areas

questions.explanation: Urbanization refers to the growth in the proportion of a country's population living in towns and cities.

questions.text: What is the name of Eritrea's second largest city?
questions.explanation: Massawa (Mitsiwa) is Eritrea's second largest city and main northern port.

questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of a capitalist society?

options.text: Means of production are owned by the state
Means of production are privately owned for profit

questions.explanation: In a capitalist society, the means of production are privately owned and operated for profit.

questions.text: In which region (Zoba) is the Bisha mine located?
questions.explanation: The Bisha gold, copper, and zinc mine is located in the Gash-Barka region.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly lists the three branches of government?
questions.explanation: The three branches of government are Legislative (makes laws), Executive (enforces laws), and Judicial (interprets laws).

questions.text: What is 'death rate'?

options.text: The number of births per 1,000 people per year
The number of deaths per 1,000 people per year
The total number of deaths in a country
The percentage of elderly people in the population

questions.explanation: Death rate is the number of deaths per 1,000 people in a population per year.

questions.text: Which of the following is the main crop grown in the lowlands and semi-arid areas of Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Sorghum is the main crop grown in the semi-arid lowlands of Eritrea.

questions.text: What does the term 'independence' mean for a nation?

options.text: Being ruled by a foreign power
Being self-governing and free from foreign control

questions.explanation: Independence means a nation governs itself without being controlled by another country.

questions.text: What is the primary function of the executive branch of government?
questions.explanation: The executive branch (led by the President and Cabinet) is responsible for implementing and enforcing laws.

questions.text: How many members does Eritrea's National Assembly have?
questions.explanation: The Eritrean National Assembly has 150 members elected to represent the population.

questions.text: Which of the following is the main environmental cause of food insecurity in Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Drought and desertification reduce agricultural productivity and cause food insecurity.

questions.text: What is 'infant mortality rate'?

options.text: The percentage of children who attend school
The number of deaths of children under 1 year per 1,000 live births
The average age of the population
The birth rate minus death rate

questions.explanation: Infant mortality rate measures the number of deaths of children under 1 year old per 1,000 live births.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT an administrative region (Zoba) of Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Tigray is a region of Ethiopia, not an administrative region of Eritrea.

questions.text: Which of the following seas is the Red Sea connected to in the south?
questions.explanation: The Red Sea connects to the Gulf of Aden in the south through the Bab-el-Mandeb strait.

questions.text: What is the name of the narrow strait at the southern end of the Red Sea?
questions.explanation: Bab-el-Mandeb ('Gate of Tears') is the narrow strait connecting the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes 'secondary industry'?

options.text: Extracting raw materials from the earth
Manufacturing raw materials into finished goods

questions.explanation: Secondary industry involves manufacturing — processing raw materials into finished products.

questions.text: What is the function of the judicial branch of government?
questions.explanation: The judicial branch interprets laws, settles legal disputes, and ensures justice.

questions.text: On a map with a scale of 1:100,000, if the map distance between two towns is 5 cm, what is the actual distance?
questions.explanation: 1:100,000 means 1 cm = 1 km. So 5 cm = 5 km.

questions.text: Which of the following describes 'immigration'?

options.text: Leaving one's country to settle in another
Entering a new country to live there
Moving within a country from one region to another

questions.explanation: Immigration is the act of moving into a foreign country to live permanently or long-term.

questions.text: Between which latitudes is Eritrea found?
questions.explanation: Eritrea is located between 12°N and 18°N latitude.

questions.text: In which year did Italy occupy the port of Massawa?
questions.explanation: Italy occupied Massawa in February 1885.

questions.text: What is the capital of Eritrea's Maekel (Central) Zoba?
questions.explanation: Asmara is the capital of both Maekel Zoba and the entire country.

questions.text: Which of the following is the capital of the Anseba Zoba?
questions.explanation: Keren is the capital city of the Anseba Zoba (region).

questions.text: The medieval title 'Bahr Negash' means which of the following?
questions.explanation: 'Bahr Negash' means 'King of the Sea' or 'Ruler of the Sea Kingdom.'

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a type of map?
questions.explanation: Physical, political, and population maps are all standard map types. 'Temporal map' is not a standard geographic map type.

questions.text: What is the name of the first Eritrean organization that worked for Eritrean rights during the British Administration?
questions.explanation: Mahber Fikri Hager (founded 1941) was the first Eritrean organization advocating for Eritrean rights.

questions.text: What is the term for the natural process by which water in the soil moves upward and evaporates from the soil surface?
questions.explanation: Evapotranspiration is the combined process of evaporation from soil and transpiration from plants.

questions.text: What are the two types of resources based on their ability to be replenished?
questions.explanation: Resources are classified as renewable (can be replenished) and non-renewable (cannot be replaced once used).

questions.text: Which of the following is a tertiary economic activity?
questions.explanation: Teaching is a service (tertiary) activity. Primary = extraction, Secondary = manufacturing, Tertiary = services.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the term 'federal state'?

options.text: A state ruled by a foreign power
A union of states with a central government sharing power with regional governments
A state with no regional governments
A state governed by military rule

questions.explanation: A federal state is a political union where power is shared between a central government and regional/state governments.

questions.text: Which of the following describes 'emigration'?

options.text: Moving to a new country permanently
Leaving one's own country to live in another
Moving between regions within the same country
Returning to one's home country after living abroad

questions.explanation: Emigration is the act of leaving one's home country to live in another country.

questions.text: Eritrea's first president is:
questions.explanation: Isaias Afwerki became Eritrea's first president after independence in 1993.

questions.text: Which of the following is the capital of the Debub (Southern) Zoba?
questions.explanation: Mendefera is the capital of the Debub (Southern) Zoba.

questions.text: Which of the following factors contributes to high population density in an area?
questions.explanation: Areas with fertile land and reliable rainfall support larger populations, leading to high density.

questions.text: The armed struggle for Eritrean independence lasted how many years (from 1961 to 1991)?
questions.explanation: The armed struggle lasted 30 years, from September 1, 1961 to May 24, 1991.

questions.text: What is the capital of Gash-Barka Zoba?
questions.explanation: Barentu is the capital of Gash-Barka, the largest region in Eritrea.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes an 'isthmus'?

options.text: A narrow strip of land connecting two larger landmasses
A body of water surrounded by land on three sides
A large elevated flat land area
An area of land surrounded by water

questions.explanation: An isthmus is a narrow strip of land connecting two larger landmasses with water on both sides.

questions.text: What is a 'peninsula'?

options.text: Land surrounded by water on three sides
A narrow strip of water connecting two seas

questions.explanation: A peninsula is a piece of land surrounded by water on three sides, with one side connected to the mainland.

questions.text: Which of the following landforms is highest in elevation?
questions.explanation: Mountains are the highest landforms, reaching peaks far above the surrounding terrain.

questions.text: What is the primary cause of rural-urban migration in Eritrea?

options.text: Too much rainfall in rural areas
Search for employment and better services in cities
Government orders to move to cities

questions.explanation: People migrate from rural to urban areas mainly in search of employment and better services.

questions.text: What is the role of a citizen in a democratic country?

options.text: To obey only the leaders they like
To participate in elections and civic life

questions.explanation: A citizen's role includes participating in elections, following laws, and engaging in civic duties.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of overexploitation of natural resources?

options.text: Planting trees to replace cut ones
Overfishing beyond what the fish population can replenish

questions.explanation: Overfishing beyond sustainable limits is a form of overexploitation that depletes fish stocks.

questions.text: What is the capital of Northern Red Sea Zoba?
questions.explanation: Massawa (Mitsiwa) is the capital of the Northern Red Sea Zoba.

questions.text: Which of the following is the capital of Southern Red Sea Zoba?
questions.explanation: Assab is the capital of Southern Red Sea Zoba.

questions.text: Which of the following ethnic groups is MOST NUMEROUS in Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Tigrinya is the most numerous ethnic group in Eritrea, comprising approximately 55% of the population.

questions.text: Which of the following is a secondary industry?
questions.explanation: Textile manufacturing is a secondary (manufacturing) industry that transforms raw fibers into finished cloth.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly identifies the role of the legislative branch?
questions.explanation: The legislative branch (parliament/national assembly) is responsible for passing and making laws.

questions.text: What does 'GDP' stand for?
questions.explanation: GDP stands for Gross Domestic Product — the total value of goods and services produced in a country.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a function of government?
questions.explanation: Governments do not directly produce food for citizens; that is the role of farmers and agricultural businesses.

questions.text: What is 'life expectancy'?

options.text: The percentage of people who survive childhood
The average number of years a person is expected to live
The age at which most people retire

questions.explanation: Life expectancy is the average number of years a person born in a given year is expected to live.

questions.text: What is the Horn of Africa?

options.text: A mountain range in East Africa
A peninsula in Northeast Africa protruding into the Indian Ocean
An island group in the Indian Ocean
A desert region in North Africa

questions.explanation: The Horn of Africa is a peninsula in Northeast Africa that juts into the Indian Ocean, including Eritrea, Djibouti, Ethiopia, and Somalia.

questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of the primitive communal society?

options.text: Class division between rich and poor
Communal ownership of everything with no private property

questions.explanation: In primitive communal society, everything is owned communally, with no private property or social classes.

questions.text: Which of the following types of farming uses large amounts of fertilizer, machinery, and irrigation?
questions.explanation: Intensive/commercial farming uses large inputs of technology, chemicals, and irrigation to maximize output.

questions.text: What is the approximate population of Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Eritrea's population is estimated at approximately 3.5 to 5 million people.

questions.text: Which of the following bodies of water separates Eritrea from the Arabian Peninsula?
questions.explanation: The Red Sea separates Eritrea (and Africa) from the Arabian Peninsula.

questions.text: What is a 'delta'?

options.text: A triangular-shaped deposit of sediment at the mouth of a river
A deep valley cut by a river
A ridge of sand in the desert

questions.explanation: A delta is a triangular-shaped area of sediment deposited at the mouth of a river where it meets the sea.

questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of a democratic government?

options.text: Power is held by a single ruler for life
Citizens have no say in governance
Citizens elect their representatives through free and fair elections

questions.explanation: In a democracy, citizens elect representatives through free, fair, and regular elections.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a non-metallic mineral resource?
questions.explanation: Salt (sodium chloride) is a non-metallic mineral resource, unlike iron, gold, and copper which are metals.

questions.text: What is the significance of September 1, 1961 in Eritrean history?

options.text: The day Italy proclaimed Italian Eritrea
The day the federation with Ethiopia was established
The day the armed struggle for independence began
The day Eritrea was liberated from Ethiopian rule

questions.explanation: On September 1, 1961, Hamid Idris Awate fired the first shots, beginning Eritrea's armed struggle for independence.

questions.text: Which of the following countries does NOT share a border with Eritrea?
questions.explanation: Yemen is across the Red Sea from Eritrea but does not share a land border with it.

questions.text: The Ottoman Turks who occupied Massawa were later replaced by which power?
questions.explanation: After the Turkish/Ottoman rule, Egypt occupied parts of Eritrea from 1872 until their defeat at Gura (1876).

questions.text: In which year was the ELF (Eritrean Liberation Front) founded?
questions.explanation: The ELF was founded in Cairo, Egypt, in 1960 by Eritrean exiles.

questions.text: What is the capital of the Northern Red Sea Zoba?
questions.explanation: Massawa (Mitsiwa) is the capital of the Northern Red Sea Zoba.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly defines 'sovereignty'?

options.text: A country's dependence on foreign aid
A country's supreme authority to govern itself without foreign interference
The power of a king to rule absolutely
A region's right to secede from a nation

questions.explanation: Sovereignty is the supreme authority of a state to govern itself and make its own decisions.

questions.text: The Battle of Afabet (1988) destroyed which Ethiopian military command?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Afabet in March 1988 completely destroyed the Nadew Command — one of the biggest EPLF victories.

questions.text: Which of the following is a method for conserving water?

options.text: Digging drainage channels to remove water
Building check dams to slow runoff and allow infiltration
Cutting down trees near water sources

questions.explanation: Check dams slow water runoff, reduce erosion, and allow water to infiltrate into the ground.

questions.text: What is the primary language of the Tigrinya ethnic group?
questions.explanation: The Tigrinya people speak Tigrinya, the most widely spoken language in Eritrea.

questions.text: What does the term 'arid' describe?
questions.explanation: 'Arid' describes a climate or region that is very dry, receiving little precipitation.

questions.text: Eritrea officially became a member of the United Nations (UN) in which year?
questions.explanation: Eritrea became a member of the United Nations on May 28, 1993, shortly after independence.

questions.text: Which of the following is the main reason why Eritrea's coastal lowlands are sparsely populated?
questions.explanation: The coastal lowlands are sparsely populated due to extreme heat and dry conditions unsuitable for farming.

questions.text: What is the official working language of Eritrea's government?
questions.explanation: Eritrea uses Tigrinya, Arabic, and English as the three official working languages.

questions.text: What does 'HDI' stand for?
questions.explanation: HDI stands for Human Development Index, which measures development using life expectancy, education, and income.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly defines 'overgrazing'?

options.text: Growing too many crops on a piece of land
Allowing too many animals to graze on land, destroying vegetation
Grazing animals in designated protected areas

questions.explanation: Overgrazing occurs when too many animals graze on land, depleting vegetation and causing soil erosion.

questions.text: Which of the following rights is a fundamental human right?

options.text: Right to have a large house

questions.explanation: Freedom of speech (expression) is a fundamental human right recognized by international law.

questions.text: Which of the following describes 'pastoral farming'?

options.text: Raising livestock for meat, milk, and hides

questions.explanation: Pastoral farming focuses on raising animals (livestock) such as cattle, sheep, and goats.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of a constitution in a country?

options.text: To list the names of government officials
To serve as the supreme law defining the rights of citizens and the structure of government
To provide a record of the country's history

questions.explanation: A constitution is the supreme law that establishes the framework of government and protects citizens' rights.

questions.text: What was the strategic importance of Massawa to colonial powers?

options.text: It was the key Red Sea port for trade and military access
It was the highest mountain in the region
It was the most densely populated city

questions.explanation: Massawa's strategic importance was as the main Red Sea port providing trade and military access to the interior.

questions.text: In which year was the Eritrean People's Liberation Front (EPLF) founded?
questions.explanation: The EPLF was founded in 1970 as a breakaway movement from the ELF.

questions.text: What is the term for the movement of people across international borders?
questions.explanation: International migration refers to the movement of people across national borders.

questions.text: Which of the following is a cause of soil erosion?
questions.explanation: Deforestation and overgrazing remove protective vegetation cover, making soil vulnerable to erosion by wind and rain.

questions.text: Which of the following Eritrean ethnic groups primarily lives in the western lowlands?
questions.explanation: The Tigre people primarily inhabit the western lowlands and northern regions of Eritrea.

questions.text: Which of the following is an effect of deforestation?

options.text: Loss of biodiversity and increased desertification
Improved water retention in the soil

questions.explanation: Deforestation leads to loss of biodiversity, increased soil erosion, and accelerated desertification.

questions.text: What does 'export' mean in trade?
questions.explanation: Exports are goods and services sold by one country to another.

questions.text: What does 'import' mean in trade?
questions.explanation: Imports are goods and services purchased from other countries.

questions.text: What is the main purpose of terracing in agriculture?

options.text: To prevent soil erosion and conserve water on slopes
To drain excess water from fields

questions.explanation: Terracing creates flat steps on hillsides to prevent soil erosion and help retain water.

questions.text: In Eritrea, which zoba (region) has the longest Red Sea coastline?
questions.explanation: The Northern Red Sea Zoba has the longest stretch of Red Sea coastline including the Dahlak Archipelago.

questions.text: What does a 'physical map' show?

options.text: Natural features such as mountains, rivers, and plains

questions.explanation: A physical map shows natural physical features such as mountains, rivers, valleys, and plains.

questions.text: What is 'trade balance'?

options.text: The total value of a country's exports
The difference between a country's exports and imports
The amount of goods traded within a country
The value of goods manufactured domestically

questions.explanation: Trade balance is the difference between the value of a country's exports and its imports.

questions.text: What is the equator?

options.text: The line at 90°N connecting the poles
An imaginary line at 0° latitude dividing Earth into northern and southern hemispheres
The line marking the International Date Line
A line connecting points of equal altitude

questions.explanation: The equator is an imaginary line at 0° latitude that divides the Earth into the Northern and Southern hemispheres.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly identifies the prime meridian?
questions.explanation: The prime meridian is at 0° longitude and passes through Greenwich, England, dividing East and West hemispheres.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes latitude lines?

options.text: Lines running from pole to pole
Lines running parallel to the equator, measuring north-south position
Lines measuring east-west distance from the prime meridian
Lines connecting points of equal temperature

questions.explanation: Latitude lines (parallels) run parallel to the equator and measure a location's north-south position.

questions.text: What is a 'savanna'?

options.text: A grassland with scattered trees, found in tropical regions
A cold desert with sparse vegetation
A dense shrubland near the coast

questions.explanation: A savanna is a tropical grassland with scattered trees and shrubs, typically found between the tropics.

questions.text: Which human social development stage is characterized by workers selling their labor for wages?
questions.explanation: In capitalist society, workers are 'free' but must sell their labor to capitalists for wages.

questions.text: What is 'nomadic pastoralism'?

options.text: Moving seasonally with herds of animals to find pasture and water

questions.explanation: Nomadic pastoralism involves moving seasonally with herds to find grazing land and water.

questions.text: What is 'infant mortality'?

options.text: Deaths of children under 1 year per 1,000 live births
Total deaths in a population per year

questions.explanation: Infant mortality rate measures the number of deaths of infants (under 1 year) per 1,000 live births.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes 'globalization'?

options.text: The process of a country becoming self-sufficient
The increasing interconnection of the world's economies, cultures, and populations
The process of countries closing their borders to trade

questions.explanation: Globalization refers to the growing interdependence and integration of the world's economies, cultures, and populations.

questions.text: What is 'per capita income'?

options.text: The total income of all workers in a country
The total GDP divided by the total population
The income of the wealthiest 10% of the population
The average wage paid in the industrial sector

questions.explanation: Per capita income is calculated by dividing a country's total GDP by its total population.

questions.text: Which of the following describes longitude lines?

options.text: Horizontal lines parallel to the equator
Vertical lines running from the North Pole to the South Pole
Lines connecting points of equal elevation

questions.explanation: Longitude lines (meridians) run vertically from the North Pole to the South Pole, measuring east-west position.

questions.text: Which of the following is the approximate length of Eritrea's Red Sea coastline?
questions.explanation: Eritrea has approximately 1,200 km of Red Sea coastline, including its many islands.

questions.text: The country that borders Eritrea in south-east is____

options.text: The Battle of Gura took place
The Battle of Dogalli took place
